_comment: H-3-CLOSURE-DESIGN.md §5 (HC-2): context anchors and prefix blocklists for the regex identifier patterns that have no check digit of their own (or whose check digit can legitimately fail on a scanned/re-typed number). Closed lists, same discipline as surname-gazetteer.json / special-category-lexicon.json — extend only via a newly measured leak + test, never speculatively. `contextAnchors` are plain regex source strings (no flags baked in; anonymizer.js compiles them with 'iu') tested for PRESENCE anywhere in a bounded backward window from the candidate match — NOT anchored to the character immediately before the candidate — and the window itself is truncated at a paragraph break (\n\n) so context never leaks across an unrelated paragraph. `contextWindow` is the window length in characters, per pattern (§5.2-§5.4).

dowodOsobisty._comment: R-DOW §5.2. blocklistPrefixes (O-HC-3): acronyms that are also valid-shaped 3-letter series prefixes and must never be emitted via the arithmetic path (A) even if the 9-char token happens to pass the check-digit formula by chance (~1/10 of random 3L+6D tokens do, R-HC-3) — defense in depth alongside the arithmetic itself. contextAnchors (path B): literal mentions of the document that let a checksum-invalid (e.g. OCR-damaged) number through anyway.

prawoJazdy._comment: R-PJ §5.3. WYŁĄCZNIE kotwiczone, nigdy bare: numeracja faktur/dokumentów księgowych ("Faktura VAT nr 12345/07/2024") ma identyczny kształt 5/2/4 — bez anchor ten wzorzec maskowałby numery faktur jako PERSON_IDENTIFIER w każdym piśmie gospodarczym.

tablicaRejestracyjna._comment: R-TR §5.4. WYŁĄCZNIE kotwiczone, nigdy bare: 2-3 uppercase letters + 4-5 alphanumerics (≥1 digit) collides head-on with currency amounts ("CHF 250 000", "USD 88812", "PLN 12345") — a bare pattern would mask amounts as VEHICLE_IDENTIFIER in every credit-agreement fragment (the exact corpus this tool is built for).
